---
title: "R Wrapper to CNCPP class"
author: "Paolo Bosetti"
date: 2026-04-30
---

# Setup and installation

Rcpp wrappers for the CNCpp `Point`, `Block`, `Machine`, and `Program` classes.

## On Windows/WSL or on Linux

You need to install R and some base packages on the linux OS:

```sh
sudo apt update
sudo apt install r-base r-base-core r-base-dev r-cran-tidyverse r-cran-rcpp
```

Then enter the R shell and **install at least one package**:

```sh
$ R
> install("adas.utils")
> q()
```

This is needed to setup the packages installation paths.

Then I suggest to install in Windows the [Positron](https://positron.posit.co/) IDE: it is a VSCode clone that has embedded R and Python interface, similar to RStudio but it can work with the intepreter running within WSL, while the GUI runs on Windows.


## On MacOS

You need a working installation of R (<https://www.r-project.org/>). Then you can use RStudio or Positron (see the above section) as you prefer.

## Wrapper installation

Install from the **repository root** after CMake has fetched the C++ dependencies:

```sh
cmake -B build -G Ninja
R CMD INSTALL R
```

## Usage


Example:

```{r}
library(cncppr)
library(tidyverse)

p <- Point(1, 2, 3)
point_length(p)

b0 <- Block("N1 G00 X0 Y0 Z0")
block_parse(b0)

b1 <- Block("N2 G01 X100 Y100 Z0 F1000", b0)
block_parse(b1)

block_summary(b1)
head(block_walk(b1))

m <- Machine()
prg <- Program("../test.g", m)
df <- program_walk(prg)
df %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=time, y=x)) +
  geom_line()

data_t <- read.csv("../test.g.csv", header = T)
data_t %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x=t_tot, y=yn)) +
  geom_line()

```
